mechanism_narrative: A sustained elevated federal funds rate above 5% increases borrowing costs and depresses equity valuations through higher discount rates, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If the 20-day forward return following any FEDFUNDS level >5% is positive in more than 55% of instances over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate from historically very low levels below 4% signals labor market slack reduction and potential wage inflation, which compresses corporate margins and leads to negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: If 20-day forward returns following UNRATE increases >0.3pp are positive in >50% of cases in a 3-year window, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous strength in retail sales and housing starts indicates excessive demand that prompts monetary tightening expectations, resulting in negative equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: If 60-day forward returns are positive in >60% of triggered months despite signal being negative, the mechanism is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: A drop in industrial production below the 100 index level signals contraction in the manufacturing sector, which rapidly transmits to broader economic weakness and negative equity returns within one week.
failure_condition: If 5-day forward returns after INDPRO <100 are positive in >50% of cases over any 2-year period, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: In a regime of very low unemployment and high federal funds rate, the economy is late-cycle and equities are highly sensitive to negative news, predicting negative 20-day returns. Both conditions are required.
failure_condition: If in triggered instances the 20-day forward return is positive in >55% of cases over a 2-year window, the hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent consumer price inflation erodes real corporate earnings and forces the Fed to maintain a tight stance, leading to negative equity returns over the following 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If in periods where CPIAUCSL is rising, the 60-day SPX return is positive in >50% of cases, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Strong real GDP growth indicates a robust economy supporting corporate earnings, and the lagged effect of past growth leads to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If 20-day forward returns following high GDPC1 are negative in >60% of quarters, the hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll gains combined with very low unemployment signals an overheated labor market that will produce wage inflation, negative for equities within a week.
failure_condition: If 5-day forward returns are positive in >55% of triggered weeks over any 2-year period, the hypothesis is rejected.
